zh.lifeSummary: John Arthur Mawde於大約1489年出生在英國越可爾哈利法可。他於1508年與Johanna Salian在英國英格蘭越可爾賀普頓斯塔爾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1566年在英國巴金哈姆爾斯福可利享年78歲。

nameMeanings.name: Mawde
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: habitational name from Mold in Flintshire, Wales, which name derives from Anglo-Norman French mont haut ‘high hill’ (Latinised as mons altus), the Norman name for Bailey Hill on which the castle stood. The name de Montalt, de Mohaut was borne by an Anglo-Norman aristocratic family who acted as castle wardens there but who had their chief seat in Riddlesden in Yorkshire, where the surname proliferated as Mawhood and Maude. In the 17th century one branch of the family acquired lands in Ireland. The Maude baronetcy of Dundrum, Tipperary, was created in 1705 for Robert Maude. His son Cornwallis Maude, Baron de Montalt, was created first Viscount Hawarden in 1793. Mowatt is the Scottish form of the name, from a different branch of the same family.

occasionally from Middle English and Norman French female personal name Mahald or Maud, which are Old French forms of ancient Germanic Mahthild (Latinised as Matilda), composed of the Germanic elements maht ‘might, power’ + hild ‘battle’. This personal name partly owed its great popularity among Normans in England to the fact that it was the name of the wife (c. 1031–83) of William the Conqueror. Surnames derived from this personal name are found in many forms and spellings, for example, Mould and Moll .

variants.name: Mall
meanings: English: from the medieval female personal name Mall, a common form of Mald or Maud. See also Maude and Moll . It had several pet forms; see Malin , Malkin , and Mallet .

variants.name: Till
meanings: English: from the Middle English female personal name Till, a pet form of Old French Matild(e) (in its Latin form Matilda), which was an alternative to Maud; see Maude 2. A diminutive form occurs in Tillotson .

variants.name: Mowatt
meanings: Scottish (northeastern): habitational name from Mold (Flintshire), see Maude 1. The distinctive Scottish form of the name has arisen from a shift of stress in Mohaut from the second to the first syllable. In Caithness and Orkney, the surname is traditionally pronounced ‘Mode’.

variants.name: Mould
meanings: from the Middle English female personal names Mold, Moud, or Molt, shortened forms of Anglo-Norman French Mahold, a common variant of Mahald; see Maude 2.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

nameMeanings.name: Arthur
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Celtic origin. King Arthur was a British king of the 5th or 6th century, about whom virtually no historical facts are known. He ruled in Britain after the collapse of the Roman Empire and before the coming of the Germanic tribes, and a vast body of legends grew up around him in the literatures of medieval Western Europe. His name is first found in the Latinized form Artorius; it is of obscure etymology. The spelling with -th- was popular among the gentry families of West Yorkshire in the late 1400s, even before Henry VII , who may have hoped to capitalize on the legend, gave the name to his son. It remained in regular use in some areas and its popularity exploded in the early 19th century, largely as a result of the fame of Arthur Wellesley ( 1769–1852 ), Duke of Wellington, the victor at the Battle of Waterloo and subsequently prime minister. Further influences were Tennyson's Idylls of the King ( 1859–85 ), and the widespread Victorian interest, especially among the Pre-Raphaelites, in things medieval in general and in Arthurian legend in particular.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: John Arthur Mawde was born about 1489, in Halifax, Yorkshire, England. He married Johanna Salian in 1508, in Heptonstall, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. He died in 1566, in Stewkley, Buckinghamshire, England, at the age of 78.

ja.lifeSummary: John Arthur Mawdeは約1489年にイギリスヨークシュアイアハリファックスで生まれた。1508年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアヘプトンスタルでJohanna Salianと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1566年にイギリスブクインガムシュアイアステウクリーで78歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: John Arthur Mawde(은)는 대략 1489년 영국 요르크실르 할이파스에서 태어났다. 그는 1508년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 헤프토스탤에서 Johanna Salian(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1566년 영국 벅잉함실르 스튜클리에서 78세의 나이로 사망했다.

Он умер в 1566 г. в местности стюкли, буккингхамшир, Англия в возрасте 78 лет.
